title: relay/state: AppState.broadcast acquires all DashMap shard locks in flat_map for large fanout
description: relay/src/state.rs:381-411: AppState::broadcast iterates the DashMap using .iter() and .flat_map() to collect all ClientTx senders. DashMap's iter() holds one shard lock at a time (not all at once), so this is correct. However: the collect() allocates a Vec<ClientTx> proportional to total connections before starting any send. For 10K concurrent connections, this is 10K Arc clones per broadcast. The subsequent join_all drives all 10K sends concurrently. If all channels are full (slow consumers), join_all blocks until all complete, not just until any complete. A single slow consumer with a full 8-item channel delays the entire broadcast. This is the correct tradeoff for fairness but warrants a comment. Documented in DESIGN.md §8 — flagging here so it's tracked.
